lines.text: DDO-LHO serial 38.49
Subject: Unidentified Flying Objects - Report of.
[unclear] valley through which runs from north, and the [unclear] from
south, is roughly 10 miles wide. The floor of the valley is between 4000
to 5000' in elevation. The east side of the valley is bordered by [unclear]
mountain (8000' in elevation), and the west side is an elongated plain
about 1000' lower than the east mountain. The blue, and all the
terrain against which these objects in quite dark in color.
objects stood out in contrast to the dark color. The objects appeared to
[unclear] in color.
The rim of the bluff on the east side of the valley is 7000' to 7500'
in elevation. From there where are the objects (at 5000' elevation) to
the bluff (7000'). From the rim of the bluff, they appeared to be
1 to 3 miles away from the bluffside, they were probably flying at about
5000' alt, and about 3000' above the valley floor.
The valley where were sighted the objects is sparsely populated.
However, there are a number of inhabitants. Many times there are instances
where [unclear] (7-2) Aeronautical Chart, Sources believe that if persons living
the valley were questioned, that instances of similar sightings of
objects from the ground. Source has flown through this valley many times
going between California and Oregon and does not recall ever seeing any
aircraft or airplane in the valley which is not a commercial air route.
Does not recall ever seeing any airplane, and no airplanes have ever been
known as such on the Boise (7-2) section of Aeronautical Chart.
First sighted and formation of objects. At the beginning of observation,
that the objects were a few degrees to the starboard of dead ahead, source
could not distinguish separate objects - all he saw were reflections.
as he came closer and passed them by 5 to 7 miles he definitely saw 6
separate objects. To be on the safe side, source states that he is positive
that there were no less than 5 objects and no more than 8.
The objects were always in this formation (one behind the other)
and there appeared to be no change in attitude of the formation. The
appeared to be about one and one half or three or four times the length
of one of the objects. The distance between the second and the following
objects was between one-half and two-thirds the length of the objects.
Accordingly, if the the objects were 20' in length, the distance between #2
and the others was between 10 and 17 feet. The distance between the objects appeared very constant
and they appeared to keep their formation rigidly in
which indicated they might have been tied by by two leading objects. At
distance of sighting, no [unclear]. If there was any, could readily not
that the objects were separate objects. The formation did not fluctuate
rapidly and steadily. Thus, source states that the objects flew very
objects they were going out of visual range on the horizon.
Weather and visibility at time of observation. Visibility was
exceptionally good. Sources notes were hazy lake, 30 miles distant, was
visible at 8000' alt. at 30 miles at 8000', Oregon, 75 miles [unclear]
there a few scattered clouds at between 12,000' and 18,000'. The sun was
